name: @forwardimpact/guide
description: Conversational agent that grounds career guidance and quality review in your organization's engineering standard.
author: D. Olsson <hi@senzilla.io>

jobs.trigger: The promotion conversation ended with 'not yet' but no specifics — unclear what evidence would change the answer.
jobs.bigHire: get guidance and evidence grounded in my organization's standard, not impressions or generic advice.
jobs.littleHire: ask a growth question and check whether recent work shows progress.
jobs.competesWith: annual reviews; mentor conversations; hoping the next project makes readiness obvious

forces.push: Career conversations feel subjective with no shared evidence base.
forces.pull: A clear picture of what's needed to close the gap, grounded in evidence.
forces.habit: Waiting for annual feedback rather than continuously self-assessing.
forces.anxiety: Structured analysis might confirm being further behind than assumed.

jobs.firedWhen: gap analysis disconnects from promotion decisions; or a reorg makes the standard feel irrelevant.

jobs.trigger: Reviewing agent output line by line because there's no way to tell whether it meets the quality bar.
jobs.bigHire: verify agent work against the standard so I review by exception, not by default.
jobs.littleHire: check expected output and get a second opinion before approving a deliverable.
jobs.competesWith: reviewing every line; accepting slower delivery; trusting uncritically; not using agents for substantive work

forces.push: Full review of every deliverable negates the productivity gain.
forces.pull: Confident delegation — knowing output meets the standard without reading every line.
forces.habit: Reviewing everything because that's how quality has always been caught.
forces.anxiety: Delegating quality judgment might miss context-specific issues.

jobs.firedWhen: verification catches fewer issues than full review; or the team stops using agents.
